reviews.review_description: In the total of three hours of playing this, I made one circle￼

Combine this with a BT-Keyboard and mouse for development and you get one of the best VR development experiences I could think of. 
Sadly the app freezes from time to time on my Quest 3 and I'm unsure if it is a quest issue or a Godot issue. However saving regularly works around this quite fine.

Hi I’ve never done godot on vr yet but I’ve done it before and in high school I told my teacher she tried it and we use it everyday  so thanks godot you are the best!

GODOT= 🤩/ amazing
I finally don't have to be mad at randoms on youtube for having a good game engine anymore

Why is it that it fills my headset with files? and even if i remove a project the files stay there and even if i were to delete them i would have to do it manually which would take ages, other than that im still learning code and this is great.
I cant uninstall this sheeeet, tried to uninstall it and it keeps downloading again! it leaves a bunch of files you have to manually delete 😤

developer_response.body: This app doesn't have any special behavior related to the installer, and if it's being redownloaded automatically for some reason it would be a platform issue. Please file a bug with Meta if that is the case. As for the other files you are having to manually delete, this app requests the MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission which allows people to create projects on the Quest file system. The app doesn't track files created by the user in this way, so it is unable to remove them when the app is uninstalled.

reviews.review_description: Develop XR projects directly on the headset!! Add an external keyboard, mouse, terminux so you can run git, and you have a totally usable development environment for 2D, 3D and XR games. Pause a running XR game and edit the code live. Its like editing reality. Mind-blowing technology
This is soooo Gayyyyyyyyyyyy
As a developer without a headset compatible with my PC this is SUPER useful! Has all the same features as PC too.

reviews.review_title: how do I use the right click?
reviews.review_description: I can't delete unnecessary files in my project

You can create your own VR game in headset! An inconvenience is that they didnt map the right click button over from mouse and computer so you get no shortcuts there. They do have a website that teaches you all about the language they use etc. Along with a few helpful Youtube videos that you can learn in one window while implimenting it in app in another! Love that convenience from Meta and Godot

I dont think theres a way we can map out the right click button ourselves so a patch for that would be extremely appreciated

reviews.review_title: Enhancing Godot XR Editor on Horizon OS
reviews.review_description:
Hello,

I want to commend the development of Godot XR Editor, as bringing Godot into a VR headset is truly fantastic. It eliminates the need for a PC to develop, allowing developers to work closer to the hardware's capabilities. Debugging becomes much simpler, making the overall experience really cool!

Given that Godot XR Editor operates on Horizon OS, designed for Meta Quest headsets, it would be very beneficial to have access to certain Meta XR SDKs, particularly the Spatial SDK, the Avatar SDK, and more. This would enrich development possibilities and optimize the user experience.

Additionally, it is unfortunate that the OS does not support more layouts for external keyboards. Currently, this limits the experience for users looking to work effectively in an XR environment.

Thank you for considering these suggestions!

A surprisingly useful port of Godot engine!
I was able to quickly put together a simple AR demo, completely inside the Quest.
And by connecting bluetooth keyboard and mouse, it really begins to shine.
I love Godot and having a native XR version is amazing! Awesome job everybody!
I don't know how to right click

reviews.review_title: Run the XR tools template so...
reviews.review_description: I've tried Godot on standalone before with issues but NOW I can run the XR tools template project from the Godot community? I cant wait to try n make small XR games/projects for game jams within the headset.

reviews.review_title: Rough around the edges, but so so cool.
reviews.review_description:
I had 20 minutes of random middle time earlier and thought I'd just download the engine onto my headset. Didn't think I'd get much further than that. But the community and the developers made sure there were some demo projects and templates available that worked right out of the box. I am a novice (and that's being generous to me) game developer who has always struggled with the settings in other engines to even get a game to run on the quest, let alone run it through the wireless as pcvr, etc etc. 

If you're like me and barely know what you're doing, just downloading it to test and go to the asset browser and find stuff. It literally took me 5 minutes total to be downloaded, installed, and up and into a demo scene using my own hands to poke things. Mind blown. I can't wait to have a bit more time to play with this later.  

Bottom line, of all the things I've done on my headset, this is absolutely the coolest. I've been testing all these prototyping apps and weird 3d modeling apps trying to capture a slice of what this application offers, and it's not even a full release.

Bravo, Mr. Huya-Kouadio (and anyone else involved!). And thank you. I think this is what will *finally* allow me to create the things I've wanted to create since I bought my first headset three years ago and was inspired by this new medium (but struggled with stupid frustrating speedbumps. Like just trying to *set up* a vr project for quest/vr in literally any of the other engines, for every single test project...-_-).

reviews.review_title: I don't know how to get it to work
reviews.review_description: 3 stars because it feels rude to give it 1 since i can't get it working. Do I need a pc for this?
I am new but so far so good  I will make my friends mod but cool I always wanted to become a official owner ￼

reviews.review_title: ⚠️ Leaves lots of files on headset !
reviews.review_description:
Warning ⚠️ 

I just deleted it as I had to but found lots of other files that have to be deleted individually!! So maybe don’t download this one yet! 


Warning ⚠️ 
It took hours to delete all the files like pictures read me files and other stuff 5000 files 


Without a keyboard u can’t test creations !/? Needs a tutorial badly ! 
but good idea in the making!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

The files you mention may be from projects downloaded from the assets library. This is an intended behavior as the editor does not delete those files to ensure users do not inadvertently lose project data.

reviews.review_description: Really wasn't expecting this, and I'm happy this exist. For me that's one step into being able to develop stuff directly inside the headset. Imagine if we had Blender to make cute small models too.

reviews.review_title: Great idea but limited use still
reviews.review_description: Having a full Godot dev environment on the quest is a great idea and works well in the enclosed space of the quest ecosystem. The big issue is lack file syncing. There is no way to easily get changed files on and off the quest as there is no GitHub or similar. None of the cloud apps such as OneDrive or google drive work on quest either.  This makes it difficult to use in the real world as you often want to either swap between the quest and pc or collaborate with other people. This isn’t the developers fault, meta just don’t have 3rd party apps needed for this. Maybe need to look at adding git support directly in the app?￼
Gorilla tag
